arts.ttl: Man charged with tragedy chanting at Liverpool’s victory over Manchester City
arts.sum: Eight more arrested on suspicion of same offence Kian Holt will appear before magistrates in January Nine men have been arrested following reports of tragedy chanting at the Premier League match between Liverpool and Manchester City on Sunday. Merseyside Police confirmed the nine, from Greater Manchester, Lancashire and Cheshire, were arrested at Anfield. A 28-year-old man from Prestwich was also arrested on suspicion of common assault. Kian Holt, 19, of Medlar-with-Wesham in Lancashire has been charged with a public order offence under the Football Offence Act 1991, namely tragedy chanting. He is due to appear at Sefton Magistrates Court on 8 January. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Man charged with tragedy chanting at Liverpool vs Man City
arts.sum: A 19-year-old man has been charged with tragedy chanting during the Premier League match between Liverpool and Manchester City on Sunday.

arts.ttl: Watch ecstastic boyhood Liverpool fan react as he is drawn against them in the FA Cup third round - as Accrington Stanley star Josh Woods prepares for the game of a lifetime
arts.sum: Woods, 24, is a forward for League Two side Accrington Stanley and is more used to assignments against Swindon Town or Newport County.

arts.ttl: Why Gary Neville and Alan Shearer will be cringing at their own words after seeing Liverpool star v Man City
arts.sum: Liverpool have had some fine players over the years of the Premier League but it’s hard to argue that any have been better – defensively...

arts.ttl: 2024/25 FA Cup third round draw: Arsenal host Man Utd; Chelsea & Liverpool draw League Two clubs
arts.sum: The two Premier League giants meet on Wednesday night and were pitted against each other in a cup tie which inspired gasps from the crowd watching the draw at United's home stadium. The top two tiers of England's footballing pyramid flooded the nation's oldest competition as the 2024/25 FA Cup third round draw was made on Monday evening at Old Trafford.

arts.ttl: Irreplaceable Virgil van Dijk remains Liverpool’s keystone and spirit animal | Barney Ronay
arts.sum: Defender’s domination of Manchester City striker Erling Haaland represents the contrasting paths of both clubs Like crocuses pushing up through the soil, like a first glimpse of daffodil in spring, you know something’s up at West Ham, and indeed Everton, when David Moyes starts turning up on Match of the Day Two like a hopeful uncle at a funeral, looking trim and urgent and chiselled, with perhaps even a slight shade of spray-salon, a touch of Ibiza Whisper. Moyes did a thorough video debrief for the BBC of Virgil van Dijk’s defending against Erling Haaland at Anfield. This wasn’t the toughest analysis given it basically involved showing Van Dijk gliding about taking the ball away, doing Dad-defender stuff – Erling, we’ve talked about this, you’ll have to wait for Crystal Palace – and generally defanging an elite striker he just seems to have the cheat code to every time. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Borussia Dortmund chief outlines Jamie Gittens plan amid Arsenal, Liverpool links
arts.sum: Borussia Dortmund managing director Carsten Cramer speaks out on rumours linking Jamie Gittens with a move to the Premier League.

arts.ttl: Mohamed Salah Open to One-Year Extension at Liverpool, per Report
arts.sum: The Egyptian star has called out the club's handling of his contract situation in recent weeks as his future remains up in the air.

arts.ttl: Liverpool rub salt in Man City's wounds with new transfer target
arts.sum: The Feyenoord winger was one of the stars in the Champions League match against Man City, in which Feyenoord came back from a 3-0 deficit to draw 3-3. "A few days ago, Hadj-Moussa kept beating one of the most praised defenders in the world, Josko Gvardiol, against Man City", the former Feyenoord player recalls.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United learn FA Cup third round opponents and face former coach
arts.sum: Newcastle United will face League Two Bromley on FA Cup third round day - managed by the Toon's former goalkeeping coach Andy Woodman. "People kind of don’t remember that.
